task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI is running and the correct EEG recording file is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is visible. The interface (likely in the playback or data source section) displays the filename 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt'.

task_completion.description: The Head Plot widget is active and visible on the dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget panel titled 'Head Plot' is open on the dashboard, displaying a top-down topographic map of a head with a heatmap.

task_completion.description: The Head Plot widget is configured to display the 'Band Power' metric.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within the Head Plot widget's settings or dropdown menus, 'Band Power' is visibly selected as the active metric or data type.

task_completion.description: The Head Plot widget is configured to the 'Alpha' frequency band (8-13 Hz).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within the Head Plot widget's settings or dropdown menus, 'Alpha' or '8-13 Hz' is visibly selected as the active frequency band.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the dashboard is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window, file manager, or image viewer shows that a file named 'alpha_topography.png' exists in the '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the authentic OpenBCI GUI interface with its standard layout, widgets, and controls, rather than a mocked interface, text editor, or web browser displaying an image.

integrity.description: The screenshot was generated by capturing the screen, not by downloading or copying a pre-existing image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history shows the use of a screenshot utility (e.g., scrot, gnome-screenshot, or GUI built-in export) rather than commands like wget, curl, or cp from an external source.

integrity.description: The screenshot was saved to the exact requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal output (e.g., from an 'ls' command) or a file manager explicitly shows the path '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/alpha_topography.png'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected recording file is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt'. The 'Head Plot' is an official OpenBCI widget that displays brain activity topography as a heatmap. The metric must be set to 'Band Power', and the frequency band must be set to 'Alpha' (which corresponds to 8-13 Hz). The final screenshot must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/alpha_topography.png'.
